title: Isachne angusta
intro: Isachne angusta, also known as narrow-leaved Isachne, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, and research has shown that it possesses several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Isachne angusta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Isachne angusta is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin disorders.
taxonomy: Isachne angusta is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This classification places it within the subfamily Chloridoideae and the tribe Cynodonteae. The genus Isachne is further grouped with other related species in the tribe Cynodonteae. Isachne angusta is a part of the larger Poaceae family, which consists of numerous species of grasses. The classification of Isachne angusta helps identify its characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plant species.
